explanation: The natural language states that default values shall be loaded when the machine is turned on and contextualizes this within 'Start-up Mode'. The LTL property G (StartUpMode -> F defaultParamsLoaded) captures this by ensuring that globally (G), whenever the system is in 'Start-up Mode' (StartUpMode), it must eventually (F) be the case that 'default parameters are loaded' (defaultParamsLoaded). This directly translates the obligation for the parameters to be loaded as part of the start-up procedure.

This LTL property captures the requirement that when the system is turned on (represented by "initStart" indicating initialization start), it must enter and remain in the "StartUpMode" until the default parameters are successfully loaded ("defaultParamsLoaded"). This ensures that the loading of parameters is a mandatory task executed as part of the initial start-up process, directly associating it with the "StartUpMode" context mentioned in the prompt. While a simpler "G (initStart -> F defaultParamsLoaded)" would ensure eventual loading, it would not explicitly enforce that this loading must occur specifically while the system is in "StartUpMode", which is implied by the structure of the natural language statement.

The natural language statement specifies that when the machine is in 'Start-up Mode', a set of default parameters shall be loaded. The LTL formula `G (StartUpMode -> F defaultParamsLoaded)` captures this by stating that globally (G), if the system is in 'Start-up Mode' (`StartUpMode`), then it must eventually (F) reach a state where the default parameters are loaded (`defaultParamsLoaded`). This ensures that the loading of parameters is a mandatory consequence of entering the start-up phase.

The natural language statement specifies that a set of default values for all parameters "shall be provided and loaded" when the machine is turned on, within the context of the "Start-up Mode." The LTL property captures this by stating that globally (G), whenever the system is in `StartUpMode`, it must eventually (F) achieve the state where `defaultParamsLoaded` is true. This ensures that the loading of default parameters is a mandatory event associated with the system's start-up process.

The natural language statement specifies that default parameters must be loaded "when it is turned on" and explicitly mentions "Start-up Mode." The atomic proposition "StartUpMode" directly represents the state of the machine being in start-up. The proposition "defaultParamsLoaded" indicates that these parameters have been loaded. Therefore, the LTL formula G(StartUpMode -> F defaultParamsLoaded) captures this requirement by stating that globally, whenever the system is in "StartUpMode," it must eventually reach a state where "defaultParamsLoaded" is true. This ensures that the loading of default parameters is a mandatory eventual outcome of the start-up process.

The LTL property "G (StartUpMode -> F defaultParamsLoaded)" states that globally, whenever the system is in 'Start-up Mode', it must eventually load the default parameters. This directly translates the natural language requirement, which specifies that the loading of default values is an action that 'shall be provided and loaded' within the context of the 'Start-up Mode' when the machine is turned on. The 'StartUpMode' atomic proposition represents the state of the system during this initial setup, ensuring that the critical step of loading default parameters is eventually completed during this phase.

The natural language statement specifies an action that "shall be provided and loaded" when the machine "is turned on", and this action is contextually defined within "Start-up Mode". The atomic proposition "initStart" accurately represents the event of the machine being turned on (system/controller initialization start). The atomic proposition "defaultParamsLoaded" signifies that the default parameters have been loaded. The "StartUpMode" atomic proposition indicates that the system is in the Start-up Mode. The LTL formula `G (initStart -> (StartUpMode U defaultParamsLoaded))` captures this by stating that globally, if the system initialization starts (is turned on), then the system must enter and remain in the `StartUpMode` until `defaultParamsLoaded` becomes true. This ensures that the loading of default parameters occurs during the Start-up Mode as a direct consequence of the machine being turned on.

The LTL property "G (initStart -> F defaultParamsLoaded)" captures the requirement that whenever the system/controller initialization starts ("initStart"), which represents the machine being "turned on", it must eventually lead to the default parameters being loaded ("defaultParamsLoaded"). The global operator "G" ensures that this condition holds true for every instance of the system being turned on. This directly translates the temporal aspect of "when it is turned on" and the future event of parameters being loaded.

The natural language statement specifies that a set of default values "shall be provided and loaded... when it is turned on" within the context of "Start-up Mode". The atomic proposition "StartUpMode" directly represents this operational mode, which is initiated when the machine is turned on. The atomic proposition "defaultParamsLoaded" indicates that the default parameters have been successfully loaded. The LTL formula `G (StartUpMode -> F defaultParamsLoaded)` translates this requirement by stating that globally (G), whenever the system is in "StartUpMode", it must eventually (F) transition to a state where "defaultParamsLoaded" is true. This accurately captures the imperative that loading default parameters is a necessary outcome during the start-up sequence.

The natural language states that a set of default values shall be loaded when the machine is turned on. The atomic proposition "initStart" accurately represents the event of the machine being turned on or system initialization starting. The property `G (initStart -> F defaultParamsLoaded)` directly translates this requirement: globally, if the system initialization starts (`initStart`), then eventually (`F`) the default parameters must be loaded (`defaultParamsLoaded`). This ensures that the critical step of loading default configurations is always completed upon system activation. The "Start-up Mode" mentioned in the text serves as the context in which this action takes place, but the core requirement is the causal link from being turned on to the parameters being loaded.
